CREATE SEQUENCE seq_test START WITH 1 INCREMENT BY 1;
CREATE TABLE test_serial (d DATE, id SERIAL, count INT AUTO_INCREMENT, info INT GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY (START WITH 100 INCREMENT BY 2 NO MINVALUE MAXVALUE 1000 CACHE 2 CYCLE));
INSERT INTO test_serial (d) VALUES (CURRENT_DATE);
INSERT INTO test_serial (d) VALUES (CURRENT_DATE);
INSERT INTO test_serial (d) VALUES (CURRENT_DATE);
SELECT id, count, info FROM test_serial WHERE count < 5;
CREATE SEQUENCE my_seq AS BIGINT START WITH 10 INCREMENT BY 5;
CREATE TABLE my_table (my_id INT DEFAULT NEXT VALUE FOR my_seq, name VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO my_table (name) VALUES ('Test1');
INSERT INTO my_table (name) VALUES ('Test2');
INSERT INTO my_table (my_id, name) VALUES (20, 'Test3');
SELECT my_id, name FROM my_table WHERE my_id > 10;
ALTER SEQUENCE my_seq RESTART WITH (SELECT MAX(my_id) + 1 FROM my_table);
INSERT INTO my_table (name) VALUES ('Test4');
SELECT NEXT VALUE FOR my_seq AS next_id;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT DEFAULT NEXT VALUE FOR my_seq, description TEXT);
INSERT INTO another_table (description) VALUES ('More test data.');
INSERT INTO another_table (id, description) VALUES (100, 'Custom ID data.');
SELECT a.id, a.description, b.my_id FROM another_table a JOIN my_table b ON a.id = b.my_id;
DROP SEQUENCE my_seq;
DROP TABLE test_serial;
DROP TABLE my_table;
DROP TABLE another_table;
